source.title: Cortical representation of the sensory dimension of pain.

confidence_rationale: This paper provides the double dissociation evidence most critical to the SD-021 claim. Hypnotic suggestions targeting pain intensity specifically modulated S1 cortical activity (sensory-discriminative dimension), while the companion Rainville 1997 paper showed that suggestions targeting pain unpleasantness specifically modulated ACC activity (affective-motivational dimension). Together they demonstrate that these two pain dimensions are neurally separable and independently modulable by top-down signals. This maps directly to the SD-021 assertion that z_harm_s (sensory-discriminative) is attenuated during commitment while z_harm_a (affective-motivational) persists. Transfer risk is moderate because hypnotic suggestion is a different top-down mechanism than E3 commitment, and S1/ACC are proximal cortical substrates rather than final spinal transmission targets.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hypnotic modulation of pain intensity (sensory-discriminative) produces specific changes in S1 activity, while modulation of pain unpleasantness (affective-motivational) produces specific changes in ACC activity. These form a double dissociation.
mapping.ree_translation: The z_harm_s / z_harm_a distinction in SD-021 maps directly onto this double dissociation. Commitment-gated descending inhibition in SD-021 selectively reduces z_harm_s PE precision (the sensory-discriminative channel) without suppressing z_harm_a (affective-motivational channel, ACC-mediated). This paper provides the cortical substrate evidence that these are genuinely separable dimensions with independent top-down modulation handles.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Hypnotic modulation engages top-down signals via expectation/suggestion, not commitment. S1 activity (cortical) is not the same as spinal-level z_harm_s PE precision; spinal gate control is the relevant locus for SD-021 and is only inferred, not measured, from the cortical signal. The double dissociation is at the cortical representation level, not at the ascending input level.
mapping.source_context: 10 volunteers; PET with tonic warm and noxious heat to hand; four conditions (alert control, hypnosis control, increased intensity suggestion, decreased intensity suggestion). Companion paper to Rainville 1997 which showed ACC modulation for unpleasantness.

confidence_components.notes: Double dissociation is the gold-standard evidence design for functional independence claims. Transfer risk is moderate: mechanism (hypnosis vs commitment) and measurement level (cortical representation vs spinal PE precision) both differ from the SD-021 claim target
